channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Apple Classifies iPhone X, Original AirPods, and HomePod as 'Vintage' Technology
item.description: Apple takes another step towards sunsetting the iPhone X, original AirPods, and HomePod by reclassifying them as vintage technology, marking their transition to obsolete status.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: EU says Meta’s paid ad-free option breaks its digital competition rules
item.description: European Union regulators accused social media company Meta Platforms on Monday of breaching the bloc’s new digital competition rulebook by forcing Facebook and Instagram users to choose between seeing ads or paying to avoid them.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Tesla deliveries are expected to fall for the second straight quarter
item.description: Tesla‘s June-quarter deliveries likely fell 3.7%, the first time the top EV maker is set to post two straight quarters of decline, as it deals with stiff competition in China and slow demand due to a lack of affordable new models.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: OpenSSH vulnerability regreSSHion puts millions of servers at risk
item.description: Researchers have uncovered a serious remote code execution vulnerability in the Open Secure Shell (OpenSSH) server that could let unauthenticated attackers obtain a root shell on servers and take them over.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: Eclipse Theia IDE arrives for desktop, browser
item.description: The Eclipse Foundation has announced the release of the Eclipse Theia IDE. Based on the Theia cloud and desktop IDE framework implemented in TypeScript, the Theia IDE is avialable for Windows, Linux, and macOS and can be tried online.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 10 voice scams to watch out for – and your quick action plan
item.description: Any potential scammer can copy a voice with cheap, accurate AI software in their bid to swindle you out of big money. Here's how to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The year so far in AI: the biggest stories from ChatGPT to Gemini and what’s coming next
item.description: It's been a huge year for AI –so at the halfway point of the year, we've rounded up the major AI products and features released in 2024 so far.
item.dc:creator: erichs211@gmail.com (Eric Hal Schwartz) , TechRadar

item.title: Windows 11 has never been so popular - but is a fresh surge of installations coming from a place of love or mere tolerance?
item.description: Almost 30% of all Windows PCs now run on Windows 11 - a slow but steady victory as more users are begrudgingly accepting the new operating system.
item.dc:creator: muskaan.saxena@futurenet.com (Muskaan Saxena) , TechRadar

item.title: Evolving hybrid cloud strategies in modern agencies
item.description: How are the CDC and TSA managing cloud adoption to meet their missions? The post Evolving hybrid cloud strategies in modern agencies first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: EU finds Meta's 'pay or consent' advertising model violates tech rules
item.description: The European Union said Meta’s “pay or consent” advertising model for European users violates the bloc’s Digital Markets Act (DMA), according to preliminary findings released Monday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: D-Link routers are being hacked to steal customer passwords — but it says there is no patch
item.description: The still-popular D-Link device in question reached end-of-life long ago, and as such will not be getting a patch.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Disgruntled VMware Customers Targeted by Nutanix's Hyperconverged Infrastructure Solutions
item.description: Nutanix, a pioneer in the hyperconverged infrastructure (HCI) market, has transformed significantly since its inception in 2011. With a focus on delivering innovative solutions to customers dissatisfied with their current VMware setup, Nutanix aims to provide a superior alternative for organizations seeking reliable and efficient HCI services.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The Supreme Court keeps on hold efforts in Texas and Florida to regulate social media platforms
item.description: The Florida and Texas laws were signed by Republican governors in the months following decisions by Facebook and Twitter, now X, to cut then-President Donald Trump off over his posts related to the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol by his supporters.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Supreme Court punts on Texas, Florida social media laws
item.description: The need for Supreme Court review grew more dire when federal appeals courts reached opposite conclusions.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court punts on Texas, Florida social media laws
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday wiped lower court rulings regarding two state laws aimed at barring social media companies from banning users based on their political views, kicking the major First Amendment challenge down the road.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, The Hill

item.title: How to trace and block anonymous calls
item.description: Do you want to prevent scam calls? Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses how to block hidden IDs on your phone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alibaba to cease data center operations in India and Australia
item.description: Alibaba’s cloud computing unit, otherwise known as Alibaba Cloud or Aliyun, has decided to cease data center operations in India and Australia.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How smaller businesses can reimagine brand building with generative AI
item.description: Generating and maintaining awareness is notoriously challenging for small businesses, often working with limited in-house resources and without the budget for external marketing support.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: What key features underpin best-in-class AI PCs?
item.description: As businesses seek to leverage AI in all aspects of their operations, it’s vital that IT leaders understand how this transformative moment will impact hardware. CIOs who aren’t actively investigating the possibilities risk being left behind.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Boeing Set to Acquire Spirit AeroSystems in $4.7 Billion All-Stock Deal
item.description: Boeing announces plans to purchase Spirit AeroSystems for $4.7 billion, bringing the supplier back under its umbrella decades after it spun off. The acquisition aims to streamline production and enhance efficiency.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, The Hill

item.title: European Union accuses Facebook owner Meta of breaking digital rules with paid ad-free option
item.description: European Union regulators have accused social media company Meta Platforms of breaching the bloc’s new digital competition rulebook by forcing Facebook and Instagram users to choose between seeing ads or paying to avoid them.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Autonomous trash-gobbling robot boat wages war on waterway waste
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson provides the details on the Class 3 Clearbot: an autonomous vessel for efficient marine trash/plant removal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why (and perhaps how) Apple Intelligence will make money
item.description: We’ll leave it to future retrospectives to show us if it turns out be a good or a bad thing, but artificial intelligence will change everything, so of course Apple is building a business around it. That shouldn’t surprise anyone.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Windows 11 Insider Previews: What’s in the latest build?
item.description: The Windows 11 2023 Update has been released, but behind the scenes, Microsoft is constantly working to improve the newest version of Windows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 7 steps to improve analytics for data-driven organizations
item.description: When leaders say they want to be a data-driven organization, a key objective is empowering business people to use data, predictive models, generative AI capabilities, and data visualizations to improve decision-making.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Instagram creators could soon make AI versions of themselves
item.description: Think you've seen them? Meta said “AI” will appear in front of the creator’s name followed by a “beta” tag after.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Will China try to make a land grab on the moon?
item.description: “A lot of their so-called civilian space program is a military program. And I think, in effect, we are in a race,” says NASA Administrator Bill Nelson.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Animal rescuers try to keep dozens of dolphins away from Cape Cod shallows after mass stranding
item.description: Animal rescuers were trying to keep dozens of dolphins away from shallow waters around Cape Cod after 125 of the creatures earlier stranded themselves.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Supreme Court nears the end of another momentous term. A decision on Trump's immunity looms
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In the last 10 days of June, on a frenetic pace of its own making, the Supreme Court touched a wide swath of American society in a torrent of decisions on abortion, guns, the environment, health, the opioid crisis, securities fraud and homelessness.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon Investigates Claims of Unauthorized Content Scraping by Perplexity AI
item.description: Amazon reviews allegations that Perplexity AI is scraping online content, including news articles from prominent sites, without proper authorization
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Navigating the future of healthcare: Transformation, measurement and integration
item.description: How will healthcare continue to transform? The post Navigating the future of healthcare: Transformation, measurement and integration first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: The Supreme Court rules for Biden administration in a social media dispute with conservative states
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday sided with the Biden administration in a dispute with Republican-led states over how far the federal government can go to combat controversial social media posts on topics including COVID-19 and election security.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Where tech, politics & giving meet: CEO Nicole Taylor considers Silicon Valley's busy intersection
item.description: Nicole Taylor has an insider's view of philanthropic trends from her seat as the president and CEO of the Silicon Valley Community Foundation.
item.dc:creator: THALIA BEATY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: AI image-generator startup Stability AI looks for reshoot with new CEO and investor group
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The troubled artificial intelligence company behind image-generator Stable Diffusion is looking for a reshoot with a new CEO and a surge of investment from a group that includes Sean Parker, Facebook’s former president and Napster's co-founder.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indonesia Resists $8M Ransom Demand After National Data Center Hack
item.description: The Indonesian government has refused to pay an $8 million ransom demanded by hackers after a cyberattack compromised its national data center, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indonesia won't pay an $8 million ransom after a cyberattack compromised the national data center
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesia's national data center has been compromised by a hacking group asking for a $8 million ransom that the government says it won't pay.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN launches global principles to combat online hate and demands big tech take action now
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United Nations chief on Monday launched global principles to combat online hate and lies and demanded that big tech companies use their power to reduce the harm they are doing to people and societies around the world.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
